key_decisions: Centralize all tools under Mad Pony Interactive Patreon — not per-tool Patreons. Reason: NotebookLM research recommends 'Human as the Brand' / Adrien Joly model. One funnel from 5K YouTube subs.
Cubric Studio Patreon = DELETE (Option A). Zero subscribers, no migration risk.
Discord description = Option C (short + punchy, mentions Cubric Studio + ComfyUI for SEO).
App name in tool itself = 'Cubric Studio' (NOT MpiAiSuite — that's only the internal repo name). User clarified.
No new ZBrush/Blender tutorials — old YouTube videos stay up but no new ones. Future tutorials = Cubric Studio, ComfyUI, AI-native dev workflows (agentic IDEs).
Gumroad = keep as passive earner. Don't migrate. Cross-sell to Patreon via bio + per-product CTAs.
Pro-tier 'all paid tools free' — delivery method TBD (Gumroad codes vs gated Patreon post). Open question.
constraints: Mad Pony brand voice = solo dev / artist / game-dev / tutorial heritage. New AI direction must EXTEND brand, not replace it. Existing Gumroad bio anchors voice: 'solo developer building tools for digital artists and game developers.'
Notebook strategy: closed-source distribution, AGPL/private repo, version lagging (Pro Day-1, Early Access -2 weeks, public -1 month).
Discord field limit = 255 chars (Option C fits).
User runs caveman mode (terse). All non-code prose stays terse.
PATH was corrupted earlier in session by setx truncation; restored via .reg file. Don't touch user PATH again.
NotebookLM CLI history command crashes on Windows cp1252 emoji rendering — use --json output to file instead, never raw stdout.
Patreon page is Next.js SSR — curl fetches return scaffolding only, no tier text. Need playwright-cli for live scrape if required.
